components.role: Common emitter gain transistor — provides voltage amplification
Collector resistor — converts collector current variations into output voltage; sets voltage gain with Re
Emitter resistor — provides DC bias stability; unbypassed portion sets minimum gain
Upper bias divider resistor — sets base DC voltage
Lower bias divider resistor — sets base DC voltage
Input coupling capacitor — blocks DC, sets input highpass cutoff
Output coupling capacitor — blocks collector DC from output
Emitter bypass capacitor — boosts AC gain by shorting Re at audio frequencies; controls gain-frequency profile

dsp_model.description: First-order highpass (coupling cap) into voltage gain stage with first-order lowpass (BJT bandwidth and Miller capacitance)

behavioral_description: A classic single-transistor gain stage with a rich harmonic character. The BJT common emitter amplifier produces predominantly even-order harmonics at moderate gain, transitioning to a mix of even and odd harmonics as it's driven harder. The emitter bypass capacitor is a powerful tone-shaping element: a large value gives full gain and a fatter bass response, while a smaller value rolls off the low-end gain boost, yielding a thinner, more trebly sound. At high gain settings (30-40dB), the stage begins to clip asymmetrically, adding pleasant grit. The inherent bandwidth limitation of the BJT provides a natural treble rolloff that prevents harshness.
